title: Alternative Uses Test - brick (Guilford 1967)
prompt: List 8 unusual, non-obvious uses of a brick. Be concrete and specific. Avoid the standard everyday use (building walls or houses). Format: one use per line.
constraint_text: 8 unusual, non-obvious uses of a brick
must_avoid: the standard everyday use of a brick: building walls and houses
retrieval_set: use a brick as a paperweight
notes: Aspects=[] is intentional - AUT has no formal aspect dictionary. With v0.2's aspect-N/A gate (when aspects=[]) and the generic creative brief, the cascade should still produce a non-trivial revision delta. We measure n_distinct_uses to detect duplicate-listing collapse.
